title: notifications-in-app — HTTP routes
description: Tenant-facing REST surface for the in-app notification bell UX. List / mark-read / dismiss / mark-all-read. All routes are Sanctum-authenticated + tenant-scoped via the tenancy:resolve middleware. The transport itself pushes over WebSockets (see broadcasts.json); these routes serve state hydration + user actions.

routes.description: List the caller's in-app deliveries. Supports spatie/laravel-query-builder filters: is_read, is_dismissed, category_slug, priority. Sorted by created_at desc by default.
Cheap endpoint for the badge — returns { data: { unread_count: 12 } }. Cached in Redis per (tenant_id, user_id) for 30s; invalidated on read/dismiss events.
Marks the delivery as read for the caller. Idempotent — second call is a no-op that still returns 200.
Bulk mark-read for every unread in-app delivery targeting the caller in the current tenant. Async-flushed via BulkMarkReadJob when count > 500.
Dismisses the delivery from the caller's bell UI. Sets dismissed_at; distinct from delete — the delivery row remains for audit.
Returns which categories the caller has in-app delivery enabled for. Read-only; write path lives on the parent notifications module.
